Politics.headline: How long can Iran survive the US's Hormuz blockade?
Politics.detail: With tensions high in the region, analysts are examining Iran's ability to withstand the economic pressure of a continued blockade.

Politics.headline: Trump's May 1 deadline: Can he continue war on Iran after that?
Politics.detail: Analysts are questioning the legal basis for continued military actions against Iran after Trump's self-imposed deadline.

Politics.headline: White House eases mandatory requirements for preserving presidential records
Politics.detail: After the Justice Department deemed a post-Watergate records law unconstitutional, the White House established a new policy that experts say weakens safeguards.

Politics.headline: Trump unveils new drug price deal with Regeneron amid affordability push
Politics.detail: President Donald Trump announced a deal with drugmaker Regeneron to lower the cost of its pharmaceutical products as part of the White House's signature drug pricing initiative.

Politics.headline: US special forces soldier charged with using classified intel to win $400,000 bet on Maduro's capture
Politics.detail: Master Sgt. Gannon Ken Van Dyke faces five criminal charges including wire fraud and unlawful monetary transactions related to betting on a raid using classified information.

Technology.headline: Apple Has a New CEO. Here's What It Means for Your Next iPhone
Technology.detail: With hardware veteran John Terunus now in charge, the iPhone could be headed for a more ambitious future, potentially influencing design and features of upcoming models.

Technology.headline: ‘Essential Voice’ Cleans Up, Translates Your Messages on Nothing Phones
Technology.detail: Nothing says research shows speaking is more natural and up to four times faster than typing on a screen, launching AI-enhanced Essential Voice first on the Nothing Phone 3.

Technology.headline: AWS, Wonder Project and Luma launch AI-powered production company
Technology.detail: Amazon Web Services (AWS) has partnered with Wonder Project and AI company Luma to launch Innovative Dreams, a filmmaker-led production services company that will pioneer Realtime Hybrid Filmmaking, combining performance capture, virtual production, and visual effects accelerated by generative AI.

Technology.headline: At Beijing auto show, Chinese carmakers flaunt new technologies as global competition heats up
Technology.detail: Chinese automakers are showcasing their latest models and technologies, including intelligent driving and ultrafast charging, at the Beijing auto show, competing with global rivals in overseas markets amid government scaling back of subsidies.

Technology.headline: Deepvein Mining Tech Wins NY Product Design Gold for Exploration Robotics
Technology.detail: Deepvein Mining Tech received Gold at the 2026 NY Product Design Awards for its Intelligent Geological Mapping and Geochemical Sampling Quadrupedal Robots, developed for mineral exploration in remote and high-risk field environments.

Science.headline: Scientists Warn About Golden Oyster Mushrooms Sold in Florida Markets
Science.detail: The golden oyster mushroom may be a culinary hit, but it's becoming an ecological problem as it spreads quickly through U.S. forests, outcompeting native fungi and reducing biodiversity. Scientists warn of its potential impact on the ecosystem.

Science.headline: New research shows land sources emit over 20 times more microplastic particles into the air than the ocean
Science.detail: Microplastics are floating through the atmosphere and spreading across the globe, but their true origins have been misunderstood. New research indicates that land sources emit significantly more microplastic particles into the air compared to the ocean.

Science.headline: Sweet discovery rewrites understanding of how our bodies store sugar
Science.detail: A recent discovery has altered the understanding of how the human body stores sugar. The details of this discovery and its implications are further discussed in the article.

Science.headline: Giant, Kraken-Like Octopuses Once Stalked Their Prey in Cretaceous Seas
Science.detail: A massive kraken-like octopus may have been the top predator prowling the seas during the age of the dinosaurs 100 million years ago. These giant octopuses stalked their prey in the Cretaceous Seas.

Science.headline: Physicists revive 1990s laser concept to propose a next-generation atomic clock
Science.detail: Physicists are revisiting a laser concept from the 1990s to develop a new kind of atomic clock. This next-generation clock could offer improved precision and stability.

Health.headline: WHO reports measurable health gains but warns of unmet goals
Health.detail: The World Health Organization (WHO) released its Results Report, highlighting measurable improvements in people's health worldwide in 2025, despite funding cuts affecting both the organization and the broader global health sector. The report indicates WHO's impact was strongest where its technical leadership and comparative advantage were fully leveraged.

Health.headline: Joint News Release: Largest catch-up initiative delivers over 100 million childhood vaccinations
Health.detail: A large-scale initiative has successfully provided over 100 million childhood vaccinations, aiming to recover ground lost due to disruptions and stagnation in global immunization coverage. This effort is crucial for protecting global health security by strengthening disease surveillance and ensuring that all children receive life-saving vaccines.

Health.headline: Epidemiological Update: Mpox in the Americas Region
Health.detail: PAHO issued an epidemiological update on Mpox in the Americas Region. PAHO continues to monitor and provide updates on various health threats in the region, including pertussis and avian influenza.

Health.headline: A 'Barbaric' Problem in American Hospitals Is Only Getting Bigger
Health.detail: Patients are experiencing extended stays in emergency departments while awaiting inpatient ward placement. This issue contributes to a 'barbaric' situation within American hospitals, highlighting the strain on the healthcare system.

Health.headline: WHO certifies the Bahamas for eliminating mother-to-child transmission of HIV
Health.detail: The World Health Organization (WHO) has certified the Bahamas for eliminating mother-to-child transmission of HIV. This achievement highlights the progress in preventing vertical transmission and improving maternal and child health.

Sports.headline: MLB: Mets survive bullpen mix-up after Bichette's 3-run double, beat Twins 10-8 for 2nd win in row
Sports.detail: The New York Mets overcame a bullpen issue following Bo Bichette's three-run double to secure a 10-8 victory against the Minnesota Twins, marking their second consecutive win. Devin Williams earned the win after the Mets' bullpen mixup.

Sports.headline: 2026 NFL draft first round highlights: Mendoza goes No. 1, Rams draft Ty Simpson
Sports.detail: The 2026 NFL draft saw QB Fernando Mendoza selected first overall, and the Rams surprised many by drafting Ty Simpson.  The draft is considered a light at the end of the tunnel for many, explaining Pittsburgh's fitting role as host.

Sports.headline: England's defensive problems mounting ahead of World Cup with 'bad' injury to versatile Livramento
Sports.detail: England is facing increasing defensive concerns as the World Cup approaches, exacerbated by a significant injury to the versatile Livramento. This adds to the challenges for the team as they prepare for the competition.

Sports.headline: Starc cleared by Cricket Australia to play IPL
Sports.detail: Mitchell Starc has been given the green light by Cricket Australia to participate in the Indian Premier League (IPL). This decision allows Starc to join the competition, boosting the excitement for cricket fans.

Sports.headline: Hurricanes beat the Senators 2-1 to take a 3-0 series lead
Sports.detail: The Hurricanes defeated the Senators with a score of 2-1, extending their series lead to 3-0. This victory puts the Hurricanes in a strong position as they progress further in the series.

Entertainment.headline: ITV TEES UP SUPERSIZED COMEDY ENTERTAINMENT SHOW, HOLEY MOLEY
Entertainment.detail: ITV announces the commission of Holey Moley, bringing the global smash hit extreme crazy golf show to the UK stage, hosted by Ant & Dec, transforming crazy golf into an adrenaline-fuelled primetime event. Contestants will tackle an epic, zany course packed with inventive obstacles, physical challenges and laugh-out-loud jeopardy.

Entertainment.headline: Dhurandhar 2 collection: Ranveer Singh's film joins all-time top 5 list, targets new record
Entertainment.detail: Ranveer Singh's 'Dhurandhar 2: The Revenge' continues its successful run at the box office, entering the top 5 highest fifth-week Hindi net earners of all time. The film's total India net collection has climbed to Rs 1,122.54 crore, with a worldwide gross of Rs 1,766.12 crore.

Entertainment.headline: Legislature scraps amusement tax on movies, concerts
Entertainment.detail: The Legislative Yuan in Taiwan has officially abolished the amusement tax on a wide range of entertainment activities, including movies, concerts, and sporting events, in an effort to encourage public participation in arts, cultural, and sports activities. The amendment removes the amusement tax on movie screenings, professional and amateur singing performances, storytelling, dance shows, circuses, magic shows, acrobatics, theater, musical performances and all types of athletic competitions.

Entertainment.headline: Choral concert to feature alumni Grammy winner
Entertainment.detail: The Fredonia School of Music's spring choral concert on April 29 will feature the Chamber Choir, the College Choir and guest choir the Westwinds, under the direction of alumnus Kent Knappenberger, who won the inaugural GRAMMY Music Educator Award in 2014. The concert is free and open to the public.

Entertainment.headline: New initiative seeks to certify that projects were made without AI
Entertainment.detail: The Human Made Mark is a new initiative that aims to give producers a way to prove to buyers and audiences that their work was created by people, not AI. The goal is to provide assurance in an industry where the use of artificial intelligence is becoming more prevalent.

Crime & Justice.headline: Exchange of gunfire inside Mall of Louisiana leaves 1 person dead and 5 wounded
Crime & Justice.detail: A shooting between two groups of people inside the food court at the Mall of Louisiana in Baton Rouge injured 10 people and at least some of those responsible fled the scene as a huge police response arrived.

Crime & Justice.headline: Macon Offenders Guilty in ATF Firearms Trafficking Investigation
Crime & Justice.detail: Three Macon offenders, two with prior felony convictions, have been held accountable at the federal level for their roles in an illegal firearms and drug trafficking network as part of a larger Bureau of Alcohol, Tobacco, Firearms and Explosives investigation in the community.

Crime & Justice.headline: US Justice Department watchdog to review Epstein files release
Crime & Justice.detail: The Department of Justice's internal watchdog announced a review of the department's compliance with the law mandating the release of the Jeffrey Epstein files, stepping into a politically sensitive saga.

Crime & Justice.headline: 1 killed, 5 injured and 5 in custody in shooting at Mall of Louisiana, police say
Crime & Justice.detail: Police said the shooting appeared to have happened after two groups of people got into an argument in the mall's food court.

Crime & Justice.headline: Two years after a judge recommended her murder conviction be tossed, Melissa Lucio still waits for freedom
Crime & Justice.detail: Two years after a judge recommended her murder conviction be tossed, Melissa Lucio still waits for freedom, caught between freedom and a potential execution.

Education.headline: Global Leaders Converge to Shape the Future of Higher Education in Asia
Education.detail: The Hong Kong University of Science and Technology (HKUST) and Times Higher Education (THE) co-hosted the Asia Universities Summit 2026 from April 22 to 24, exploring Asia's pivotal role in reshaping global innovation and addressing societal challenges through higher education.

Education.headline: Replicating Bologna process for APAC 'incredibly difficult'
Education.detail: Experts suggest that replicating the Bologna Process, a series of agreements to standardize higher education systems across Europe, for the Asia-Pacific region would be incredibly difficult.

Education.headline: Engineering without the liberal arts is a broken machine
Education.detail: An engineering student at the University of Connecticut argues that a liberal arts education, which teaches students *how* to think rather than *what* to think, ought to be a foundational pillar of engineering education, enhancing problem-solving skills with deeper discussions about the problems being solved.

Education.headline: UConn Rec ends funding for club sports, cuts ties with multiple teams
Education.detail: UConn Rec has decided to end funding for club sports, resulting in severed ties with multiple teams.

Education.headline: HKUST and Times Higher Education Co-Host Asia Universities Summit 2026
Education.detail: The Hong Kong University of Science and Technology (HKUST) and Times Higher Education (THE) co-hosted the Asia Universities Summit 2026 from April 22 to 24, exploring Asia's pivotal role in reshaping global innovation and addressing societal challenges through higher education.

Lifestyle.headline: 10 tips for being a smarter farmers market shopper
Lifestyle.detail: Peak farmers market season is coming, and here's how to make the most of it with these helpful tips.

Lifestyle.headline: Experience: I've won £1m on the lottery – twice. now.
Lifestyle.detail: Richard Davies recounts the experience of winning the lottery twice.

Lifestyle.headline: Nita Ambani's 'tribal lore' Bengali heirloom sari at 2026 TIME100 summit took 24 months to weave.
Lifestyle.detail: Nita Ambani wore a special Bengali heirloom sari that took two years to weave at the TIME100 summit.

Lifestyle.headline: Twice's Dahyun rejoining group mates at Tokyo concert, while Chaeyoung hints at comeback.
Lifestyle.detail: Dahyun of the group Twice is set to rejoin her bandmates at a concert in Tokyo, and Chaeyoung has hinted at a possible comeback.

Lifestyle.headline: Dentist reveals 6 signs in the mouth that can predict colorectal cancer risk.
Lifestyle.detail: A dentist shares six oral signs that could potentially indicate the risk of colorectal cancer.

Opinion/Editorial.headline: Opinion: Rolling back Colorado's right-to-repair laws will weaken critical infrastructure safety
Opinion/Editorial.detail: Senate Bill 90 would tighten control over who can service systems while leaving manufacturers free to continue shipping insecure ones, and in crisis conditions, it could slow response and recovery at the moment speed matters most.

Opinion/Editorial.headline: The costs of Trump's contempt are starting to show
Opinion/Editorial.detail: Europe is moving from words to actions as anti-Americanism grows in response to Trump's policies and behavior, with the European Union planning to invest heavily in defense and seeking alternatives to U.S.-dominated financial platforms.

Opinion/Editorial.headline: Guest Editorial: Dark side of U.S.-Israel alliance
Opinion/Editorial.detail: Netanyahu's policies are fueling anti-Semitism, and the U.S.'s unwavering support enables his actions, while Trump's decision to scrap the Iran nuclear pact has further destabilized the region.

Opinion/Editorial.headline: Inflation won't stop at pump - Editorial
Opinion/Editorial.detail: Fuel hikes pose a danger to the squeezed middle class, whose forced spending cuts could trigger a much broader economic slowdown by weakening domestic demand and slowing overall economic growth.

Opinion/Editorial.headline: Mark Kelly's 180 days of socialism
Opinion/Editorial.detail: Editorial Board opines on Mark Kelly's policies.

World.headline: Trump orders US military to 'shoot and kill' Iranian small boats choking Strait of Hormuz
World.detail: Amid rising tensions, President Trump has instructed the U.S. military to engage and neutralize Iranian small boats in the Strait of Hormuz. This order comes after Iran seized two container ships in the same area.

World.headline: Global hunger report warns of rising malnutrition and famine risks
World.detail: A new report highlights that conflict and violence in 2025 caused acute hunger for nearly 150 million people, with famine confirmed in parts of the Gaza Strip and Sudan. The risk of famine extends into 2026 for areas within Gaza, Sudan and South Sudan.

World.headline: Establishing an International Panel on Inequality
World.detail: President Cyril Ramaphosa and global experts met at Wits University to establish an International Panel on Inequality, addressing the global inequality emergency where one in four people worldwide regularly skip meals, while billionaire wealth has reached record levels. The panel was proposed in a report for South Africa's G20 Presidency and is supported by multiple countries.

World.headline: US soldier charged with using Polymarket to bet on Nicolas Maduro abduction
World.detail: An active-duty U.S. soldier, Gannon Ken Van Dyke, has been criminally charged by the Department of Justice for allegedly using classified military information to place bets on the abduction of Venezuelan President Nicolas Maduro, profiting over $400,000. He allegedly used the Polymarket platform for these bets.

World.headline: Syrian authorities arrest suspected leader of notorious Tadamon massacre
World.detail: Syrian authorities announced the arrest of a former intelligence officer who appeared in a leaked video from four years ago, allegedly showing him and others fatally shooting dozens of people during the Syrian conflict. The suspect is believed to be a leader in the Tadamon massacre.

Zimbabwe.headline: Manica Diamonds FC new owner seeks ZIFA approval to disband senior team
Zimbabwe.detail: Africa Mineral Ventures, the new owners of Manica Diamonds FC, have requested permission from the Zimbabwe Football Association (ZIFA) to disband the club's senior team, proposing to merge their Division One side with the Manica Diamonds junior team, after paying the players and technical team salary advances.

Zimbabwe.headline: Chatunga Mugabe and co-accused paid shooting victim R250,000 cash to silence him – court hears
Zimbabwe.detail: Chatunga Mugabe, son of the late former President Robert Mugabe, and his co-accused allegedly paid R250,000 to the victim of a shooting incident in Johannesburg to silence him, according to court proceedings.

Zimbabwe.headline: Minister Polite Kambamura Cracks Down on Proxy Mining Deals, Warns of Imminent Policy Shift
Zimbabwe.detail: Minister Polite Kambamura is taking a firm stance against proxy mining deals, signaling a forthcoming policy shift to enhance transparency and accountability in the sector.

Zimbabwe.headline: Ambulance Carrying Patient Crashes with Land Rover in Harare
Zimbabwe.detail: An ambulance transporting a patient was involved in a collision with a Land Rover in Harare, resulting in unconfirmed injuries.

Zimbabwe.headline: Mwonzora drags police to court over ban on anti–Constitutional Amendment Bill No. 3 protest
Zimbabwe.detail: The MDC is challenging the police's decision to prohibit their planned protest against Constitutional Amendment Bill No. 3, citing a violation of citizens' rights to assembly and expression.
